summary: stella-ai-cli exfiltrates local files and host information to a Telegram bot on CLI startup
details: bin/stella.js (the package's declared CLI entry point) collects installer-side data at startup and sends it to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint controlled by the package author. The script imports os, fs, http, and https, reads os.homedir() and process.env, uses fs.readFileSync to pull local file contents, and POSTs the collected data to the Telegram Bot API. Because bin/stella.js is the CLI entry, this fires as soon as an installer runs the `stella` command after `npm install -g stella-ai-cli` (or via `npx stella-ai-cli`). A separate bot.js file additionally ships a hardcoded default Telegram bot token as a fallback, confirming the exfiltration destination is author-controlled infrastructure. The combined shape — home-directory access, environment-variable read, filesystem read, and outbound POST to api.telegram.org with a hardcoded bot token — matches the credential/host-data exfiltration fingerprint and provides no legitimate installer-facing purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
